summary: All 14 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. 5 frontmatter code_refs all SUPPORTED at or within a few lines of cited locations (note frontmatter line numbers are now accurate: viva_filter.py:58 and rnnoise_denoiser.go:41 are the actual class/func locations). All 7 Vapi Problem-#2 figures (3s/20ms windows, 85th pct, 100ms exp smoothing, media detection, 500ms grace, -35dB fallback, quieter-than-speaker quote) match _sources/blogs/vapi-pipeline-part-2.md verbatim. Cloudflare 'no denoiser' confirmed by grep returning no matches across the cloudflare-agents clone. One minor imprecision (not downgrading): note prose says RNNoise 'always resamples to 48 kHz' but code only resamples when input != 48kHz; it always TARGETS 48kHz, and the code comment literally says 'RNNoise always requires 48kHz.' Source shortname vapi-pipeline-2 resolves to the file named vapi-pipeline-part-2.md.

claims.found: class KrispVivaFilter(BaseAudioFilter): at L33; __init__ defaults frame_duration: int = 10 (L44), noise_suppression_level: int = 100 (L45). Cited L35 is inside the class docstring, within window.

claims.claim: Pipecat RNNoiseFilter, requires 48kHz, default resampler_quality=QQ for lowest latency
claims.found: class RNNoiseFilter(BaseAudioFilter): at L30; resampler_quality: SoxrQuality = 'QQ' (L39) 'Defaults to QQ (Quick) for lowest latency'; '# RNNoise always requires 48kHz.' self._rnnoise = RNNoise(sample_rate=48000) (L69-70)

class KrispVivaFilterFrameProcessor(rtc.FrameProcessor[rtc.AudioFrame]): at L58; docstring 'used directly with the noise_cancellation parameter in AudioInputOptions or RoomInputOptions'; example shows noise_cancellation=processor in AudioInputOptions (L84)

claims.claim: Rapida GetDenoiser() switches on microphone.denoising.provider: krisp->Krisp, default->rn_noise
claims.found: func GetDenoiser(...) at L27; provider, _ := options.GetString(DenoiserOptionsKeyProvider) key='microphone.denoising.provider' (L23); switch case KRISP -> NewKrispDenoiser, default -> NewRnnoiseDenoiser (L29-34)

claims.claim: Rapida RNNoise denoiser config is 48kHz linear16 (NewRnnoiseDenoiser at :41, config at 72-74)
claims.found: func NewRnnoiseDenoiser(...) at L41; denoiserConfig: &protos.AudioConfig{ SampleRate: 48000, AudioFormat: protos.AudioConfig_LINEAR16 } (L72-74)

claims.claim: Baseline tracking: RMS amplitude over 3-second rolling windows in 20ms chunks
claims.found: L21: 'Baseline Tracking: RMS amplitude over 3-second rolling windows using 20ms chunks.'

claims.claim: 85th percentile of audio-level distribution becomes filtering threshold
claims.found: L22: 'Dynamic Thresholds: 85th percentile of audio-level distribution as filtering threshold.'

claims.claim: baseline updated every 100ms via exponential smoothing
claims.found: L23: 'Continuous Adaptation: update baseline every 100ms via exponential smoothing.'

claims.claim: media detection -> more aggressive filtering with auto-adjusted baseline offset
claims.found: L24 'Media Detection: switch to more aggressive filtering when consistent background audio detected.' + L25 'baseline offsets auto-adjust when TV/music detected.'

claims.claim: 500ms grace period prevents clipping the start of words
claims.found: L25: 'Add 500ms grace period to avoid cutting off the start of words.'

claims.claim: static fallback at -35 dB acts as a floor
quote: 'background speech is typically quieter than the primary speaker'
claims.found: L25: 'Core insight: background speech is typically quieter than the primary speaker.'

claims.claim: Cloudflare ships no denoiser; voice-providers bridge telephony without preprocessing
claims.found: voice-providers dir holds telnyx/deepgram/elevenlabs/twilio; grep -ril 'denois|noise_cancel|rnnoise|krisp' across cloudflare clone returned zero matches

claims.claim: RNNoise always resamples to 48 kHz since RNNoise requires sample_rate=48000
claims.found: rnnoise_filter.py L69-70 '# RNNoise always requires 48kHz.' RNNoise(sample_rate=48000); L77 'if self._sample_rate != 48000' enables resampling. Minor: resampling only when input != 48kHz, but target is always 48kHz.
